content.text:
MEMORY-ONLY MAINTENANCE TASK

                This is a private memory pipeline call, not a normal assistant reply.
                Do not answer as a chat assistant.
                Do not call tools.
                Execute only the maintenance stage below.

                Output contract:
                Return exactly one valid JSON object matching the response schema.
                No prose, no markdown fences, no explanation.

                        You are NoteConsolidator v2 for a long-term AI agent memory system.

        Goal:
        Review selected notes and decide whether any of them should now produce durable structured memory.

        Current time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Selected notes:
        - id=hot-path:note:<0>; type=LESSON; status=ACTIVE; maturity=FRESH; importance=8; confidence=0.98; title=NoMemoryVerifier lesson: verify ambiguous no-memory recall decisions with a model call; summary=In Memory MVP, when ReadTimeRetrievalPlanner returns need_memory=false on ambiguous recall questions, NoMemoryVerifier should run a short model-based verification before accepting the no-memory plan and avoid hard-coded substring heuristics.; scope=Applies to Memory MVP retrieval-planning behavior for ambiguous recall intent decisions.; anchor=entity:<0>; entities=entity:<1>;entity:<2>;entity:<3>; evidence=chat:<0> for ambiguous no-memory recall decisions, prefer a cheap model-based verifier over substring matching.; candidate_claims=[]
- id=hot-path:note:<1>; type=LESSON; status=ACTIVE; maturity=FRESH; importance=8; confidence=0.97; title=SourceHydrator lesson: bounded quote hydration on evidence fallback; summary=In Memory MVP, SourceHydrator should retrieve a small bounded set of source quotes only when evidence fallback is requested, after interpreted claims are retrieved, to keep answers grounded without overloading the runtime prompt.; scope=Applies to Memory MVP SourceHydrator source-evidence hydration strategy.; anchor=entity:<4>; entities=entity:<5>;entity:<6>;entity:<7>; evidence=chat:<1> for source evidence hydration, prefer bounded quote hydration over dumping all sources into the runtime prompt.; candidate_claims=[]

        Entity catalog:
        - id=entity:<0>; type=CONCEPT; name=NoMemoryVerifier; summary=Concept named NoMemoryVerifier.
- id=entity:<8>; type=CONCEPT; name=ReadTimeRetrievalPlanner; summary=Concept named ReadTimeRetrievalPlanner.
- id=entity:<9>; type=PROJECT; name=Current project; summary=Project named Current project.
- id=entity:<10>; type=PROJECT; name=Memory MVP; summary=Project named Memory MVP.
- id=entity:<4>; type=SERVICE; name=SourceHydrator; summary=Service named SourceHydrator.

        Related active claims:
        none

        Related active notes:
        none

        Related tasks:
        none

        Existing profile:
        none

        Return JSON:
        {
          "claim_candidates": [],
          "task_actions": [],
          "profile_patch": null,
          "episode_candidates": [],
          "note_actions": [
            {
              "note_id": "note-id",
              "action": "keep_active | mark_resolved | mark_stale | supersede | mark_consolidated",
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ],
          "summary": "short summary"
        }

        Rules:
        - Consolidate only when the signal is strong enough.
        - Do not force every note into a claim.
        - Promote to claim when the meaning is stable, queryable, and precise enough.
        - Promote to task only when future action is clearly required and already represented by the selected note.
        - Return one episode_candidate for each selected LESSON note that clearly contains a reusable situation-action-result-lesson pattern.
        - Do not collapse several unrelated selected lessons into one episode_candidate.
        - For episode_candidates, owner_entity_id should be the most specific component/service/person/concept the lesson applies to. Use the project entity only when no more specific owner exists.
        - If a selected note is a useful stable rule but not reusable experience, promote it to claim without creating an episode_candidate.
        - Write claim text, task text, profile text, episode text, and reasons in English.
        - Preserve proper names, component names, repo names, exact identifiers, dates, commands, and quoted terms.
        - Every claim candidate must include origin_note_id from Selected notes.
        - Use only entity ids from Entity catalog.
        - If no entity is better, use the selected note anchor entity.
        - Use object_entity_id for entity objects and object_value_json for scalar/string objects, never both.
        - evidence_quote may be null for derived memory; provenance will use the origin note evidence refs.
        - Existing claims and tasks are dedup/context only.
        - If a selected note already has a matching active claim or task, prefer note_actions with mark_consolidated or keep_active instead of duplicating memory.
        - Use mark_consolidated only when durable memory was created or already exists.
        - Use keep_active for unresolved rationale, hypotheses, weak ideas, or notes that remain useful as notes.
        - Return valid JSON only.

responseFormat.description: Consolidate mature notes into durable memory objects and note lifecycle actions.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_consolidator

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

matching.","origin_note_id":"hot-path:note:<0>","owner_entity_id":"entity:<0>","reason":"Clear reusable situation-action-result pattern for a specific component.","result":"Lower risk of incorrect no-memory outcomes on ambiguous recall intent while keeping verification lightweight.","situation":"In Memory MVP, ambiguous recall questions were being classified by ReadTimeRetrievalPlanner with need_memory=false.","success_score":0.9,"tags":["memory-mvp","retrieval-planning","verification","heuristics","no-memory"]},{"action":"Hydrate only a small bounded set of quotes, and only when
